task.title: Rejoin tight continuations and delimiter adjacency
task.summary: A new batch of fixture edits shows several small but important formatter gaps where tight syntactic continuations stay awkwardly split or gain spaces that do not belong.
task.problem: Current fixture residue shows a few distinct formatter-scope issues. Optional typed parameters can drift from `instance_name?: string` to `instance_name? : string`. Simple boolean or comparison continuations can remain broken as `where type` followed by `== dir` on the next line. Grouped pipeline expressions can preserve a bare `|` on its own line instead of rejoining to the surrounding expression. And inline grouped expressions or blocks can keep extra delimiter-adjacent spaces or line breaks such as `( open ...` or `else { "http"\n}` when the expression should settle back into a compact house style.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Optional typed parameter syntax preserves the house form around `?` and `:`, keeping `instance_name?: string` rather than `instance_name? : string`.
Simple comparison continuations that still fit on one line rejoin into a normal expression, including shapes like `where type == dir`.
Grouped single-expression pipelines do not preserve a bare `|` on its own line when the surrounding expression should rejoin compactly.
Delimiter adjacency is normalized for compact grouped expressions and inline blocks, removing unwanted spaces after `(` and rejoining trivial inline block bodies when they fit.
Regression coverage is drawn from the real fixture residue in `lima-ports.nu` and `download-latest-iso.nu`.
